title: Qutrit Clifford+T gates by two-body angular momentum couplings, rotations and one-axis-twistings
abstract: We develop an angular momentum representation and implementation of the Clifford+T set of unitaries for qutrits. We show that local gates from this set can be realized by the sole use of suitable rotations and one-axis-twisting operations, which are at most quadratic in the angular momentum operators and thus can be experimentally realized in many quantum systems. Controlled rotations are shown to only require linear angular momentum couplings and, as a consequence, the full qutrit Clifford+T set is shown to be expressed solely in terms of two-body angular momentum couplings, rotations and one-axis-twisting operations. By employing the Jordan-Schwinger map, we show an analogous implementation in terms of bosonic modes, improving on the number of modes with regard to a previous scheme. Moreover, we employ the cross-Kerr interaction in order to obtain any qutrit Clifford+T gate for bosonic modes. We illustrate our findings with simple schemes for preparing entangled states of interest.
summary: This paper presents a theoretical framework for implementing the complete Clifford+T gate set for qutrits. It shows that these gates can be constructed using only two-body angular momentum couplings, rotations, and one-axis-twisting. The authors also provide an efficient mapping to bosonic systems, reducing the required number of modes per subsystem. Finally, the work demonstrates how these gates can be used to deterministically prepare complex entangled and graph states.
main_problem: Developing a physical implementation and representation for the qutrit (d=3) Clifford+T gate set using low-order angular momentum couplings and bosonic modes.
main_result: The full qutrit Clifford+T set can be realized using only two-body angular momentum couplings, rotations, and one-axis-twisting operations, with an improved bosonic mode requirement of only two modes per subsystem.
method: The authors use an angular momentum representation (j=1) and the Jordan-Schwinger map to translate gates into bosonic mode operations, decomposing gates into sequences of rotations, one-axis-twisting, and Kerr nonlinearities.
model_or_system: The study considers spin-1 (j=1) angular momentum systems and two-mode bosonic systems (e.g., optical interferometers, BECs, or circuit QED) utilizing self-Kerr and cross-Kerr interactions.
important_parameters: The magic angle alpha = tan^-1(sqrt(2)) for the Theta_z operator, and the edge weights phi_e for angular momentum graph states.
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes an idealized, lossless scenario and uses h-bar = 1 for simplicity.
figures_summary: An interferometric diagram illustrating the preparation of a qutrit angular momentum graph state using two-photon states as input.
paper_structure: The paper introduces an angular momentum representation for qutrit gates, demonstrates the decomposition of the Clifford+T set into rotations and one-axis-twisting, maps these to bosonic modes via the Jordan-Schwinger map, and concludes with applications to entanglement and graph state preparation.
why_interesting: This work provides a practical blueprint for scaling quantum computing from qubits to qutrits using standard nonlinear interactions like Kerr nonlinearities, which is highly relevant for quantum optics and circuit QED.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1.1: Interferometric diagram for the preparation of a qutrit angular momentum graph state |JGHZ⟩. The input state in each subsystem is the two-photon state |2, 0⟩.
